preamble: The emergence of a voiced palatal plosive (ɟ) from an empty sound or zero is a documented phonetic transformation, primarily occurring through the process of epenthesis. This involves the insertion of a consonant, often to resolve hiatus (a sequence of vowels) between morphemes or words, or as the surfacing of a historically latent segment.
phoneticEffects: This transformation is a form of consonant epenthesis, specifically the insertion of a voiced palatal plosive (ɟ)

examples.note: Historically driven insertion of [ɟ] to resolve hiatus, particularly after bases ending in /e/, as a relic from Middle (Pahlavi) Persian. For example, the word /bande/ 'servant' in Middle Persian could historically trigger [ɟ] epenthesis when followed by a vowel-initial suffix.
Epenthetic [ɟ] (which can vary to [ɟʝ], [ʝ], or [j]) inserted between the 3rd person prefix 'i-' and a vowel-initial root to avoid hiatus.
